title: ostericum palustre
intro: Ostericum palustre, also known as Chinese angelica or dong quai, is a perennial plant native to Asia and Europe, belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The medicinal properties of ostericum palustre are attributed to its high concentration of iridoid glycosides, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to promote women's health, alleviate menstrual cramps, and support the immune system, among other health benefits. Ostericum palustre can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, capsules, and tinctures, which are commonly used to support menstrual health, reduce inflammation, and enhance overall well-being.
